title: Weather warning: Damaging winds
description: GARDEN ROUTE NEWS - A Level 2 weather...
bodyText:
GARDEN ROUTE NEWS - A Level 2 weather warning has been issued for the Western Cape due to damaging winds.

A cold front is expected to result in strong winds between Saldanha and Cape Agulhas ( 40 to 60 km/h) and it will spread to Plettenberg Bay towards the evening on 3 July. Waves heights of 3.5 to 4m are expected which will result in rough and choppy seas.


> These winds, capable of causing significant disruption and damage, may lead to travel delays, infrastructure damage, and hazardous outdoor conditions.


Residents are advised to avoid non-essential travel, secure loose objects, and stay indoors while following updates from local authorities. Stay tuned to local news and official weather services for further information. Stay safe and take necessary precautions.


> Impact:


- Travel Disruption: High winds may lead to road closures and delays in public transportation.
- Infrastructure Damage: Potential damage to buildings, trees, and power lines.
- Hazardous Conditions: Increased risk of flying debris, making outdoor activities dangerous.


> Advice:


- Avoid non-essential travel.
- Secure loose objects around your property.
- Stay indoors where possible and follow updates from local authorities.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
